steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: The cleft construction with the pattern [noun] is what has ______ requires the blank to be filled with a past participle (following the auxiliary “has”) plus its objects to complete the relative clause.

steps.title: Apply the grammar and punctuation rule
steps.content:
A verb phrase with auxiliary “has” requires a past participle in standard word order (verb → indirect object → direct object), and a declarative sentence ends with a period.

Choice D correctly applies this rule with “earned him his enduring fame.” The other three choices violate this rule: A and B end with question marks (contradicting the declarative mood), and C places a noun before the verb (“his enduring fame earned him”), violating the cleft construction structure.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
